verdicts.missing: Association is replaced by physical touching, so the act is merely detachment from contact.
verdicts.reason: It describes someone making two touching things cease to touch, not removing something from an association.

verdicts.missing: It lacks an intentional removing act and replaces association with physical nearness.
verdicts.reason: It renders only an event in which two things cease to be near one another.

verdicts.missing: Association is reduced to spatial nearness, yielding physical separation instead of dissociation.
verdicts.reason: Although an agent causes the change, the criterial prior association is represented only as being near.

verdicts.missing: Association is replaced by spatial nearness, so the action described is physical removal.
verdicts.reason: It sharply represents an agent-caused cessation of nearness but not removal from association.
It represents an event caused by someone whereby something formerly acting with another no longer does so, adequately rendering removal from association.

verdicts.missing: Association is replaced by mere location among many people, and the resulting exclusion is not explicitly caused by the act.
verdicts.reason: It describes moving something out of a crowd or social location rather than ending its association.

verdicts.missing: Association is replaced by spatial nearness, and the action is not explicitly linked as the cause of its cessation.
verdicts.reason: The before-and-after pattern captures separation but not specifically the act of removing from association.
